name: aws-content-plugin
description: AWS cloud content creation plugin — presentations, architecture diagrams, animated diagrams, documents, GitBook sites, and workshops

hooks.command: bash -c 'if echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | grep -q "remarp_to_slides.py"; then if echo "$TOOL_OUTPUT" | grep -qi "warning\|error"; then echo "Build warnings detected. Review output before proceeding."; fi; fi'

bash -c 'FILE="$TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH"; if [[ "$FILE" == *reactive-presentation/references/* ]] || [[ "$FILE" == *reactive-presentation/SKILL.md ]] || [[ "$FILE" == *reactive-presentation/scripts/* ]]; then echo "[SKILL CHANGED] reactive-presentation skill file modified: $(basename $FILE). Run skill-dev-pipeline test to verify."; fi'

bash -c 'FILE="$TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH"; if [[ "$FILE" == *.md ]] && head -5 "$FILE" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "remarp: true"; then python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open(sys.argv[1]).read(); slides=t.split(chr(10)+\"---\"+chr(10)); missing=[i+1 for i,s in enumerate(slides) if s.strip() and \":::notes\" not in s and \"---\" in t]; print(f\"Slides missing notes: {missing}\") if missing else print(\"All slides have notes.\")" "$FILE"; fi'

SessionStart.prompt: AWS Content Plugin loaded. Quality gate (content-review-agent >= 85) is mandatory before deployment.
